question_text: Explain why ice skates allow skaters to move faster than running shoes on ice.
correct_answer: Ice skates have smooth blades that create less friction with the ice compared to the rough surface of running shoes, so there is less resistance to motion.
explanation: The smooth, thin blade of an ice skate reduces the contact and roughness between the skate and ice, creating much less friction. Running shoes have a rough, textured sole that creates more friction and grips the ice, slowing you down.
